title: Vendor SDMMC0 power and card-detect policy audit
trigger: Close vendor SDMMC0 power, regulator, and card-detect policy as a possible missing board-level runtime requirement.
findings: Vendor SDMMC0 leaves non-removable, broken-cd, cd-gpios, vmmc/vqmc/vdmc, and sunxi-power-save-mode commented out.
BSP card-pwr-gpios handling exists, but vendor SDMMC0 does not use an active card-pwr-gpios property.
BSP cd-used-24M and data3-detect paths are DT-boolean gated; vendor SDMMC0 does not enable them.
The local Cubie A7S DTS provides a fixed always-on 3.3V vmmc supply and marks SDMMC0 as SD-only with no-mmc and no-sdio.
Runtime evidence already proves card init, pre-mmcblk CMD17, small PIO CMD18, partition discovery, and read-only EXT4 root mount with IDMA bypassed.

public_search.notes: Search still did not reveal a public archive hit or answer for the manually sent A733 SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch question. Radxa public docs confirm Cubie A7S has a microSD slot, but provide no IDMAC descriptor-fetch clue.

reason: Power and card-detect policy can affect card presence or card initialization, but those stages already work. The remaining failure is normal-IDMAC memory reachability before descriptor word 0 is consumed.
next: Do not add vendor card-detect or card-power properties to the upstream DTS as an IDMAC fix.
Keep waiting for a maintainer/list reply or a source-backed SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission bit.
